name: Commit Lineage
description:
Commit-attribution feed (/commits) — 'which Claude Code session produced which commit'. Customer-facing home for the lineage view that previously lived only in the dev-only qontinui-supervisor (its Lineage tab). The page body (components/commits/CommitLineage.tsx) reads from the web backend's commit-lineage proxy (/api/v1/operations/lineage/* -> coord.commit_lineage). Layout: a page header (GitCommitHorizontal icon + h1 'Commit Lineage' + a 'Which Claude Code session produced which commit' subtitle), a toolbar (last-updated text + a 'Refresh' button), four stat tiles (Commits / Sessions / Repos / By source), a 'Top sessions' Card with a table (Session / Commits / Last commit), and a 'Recent commits' Card with a table (Commit / Repo / Branch / PR / Session / Source / When). Session names render as clickable CommitSessionChip pills (components/commits/CommitSessionChip.tsx) that open a shadcn Dialog listing that session's commits.

This spec asserts ONLY the data-independent structural chrome that renders regardless of how much lineage data the backend returns: the page container, the header heading + subtitle, and the toolbar Refresh button. The two tables (Top sessions / Recent commits) render their <table> + column-header cells ONLY when the backend returns >=1 row; at zero rows the component swaps in a plain empty-state <div> ('No session attribution yet.' / 'No commit lineage recorded yet.'). Spec-CI runs against a hermetic empty database with no live coord, so the populated-table slices are NOT reachable there. The 'commits-tables-populated' state captures the table structure for when this spec is run against a populated backend; recommend_state selects it only when the live page actually rendered tables, so it never forces a CI failure. The chip-opens-dialog flow is interaction-gated; it is documented here as the 'commits-session-dialog' state (structure only, no transition authored) and is exercised by component-level coverage rather than the transition executor until a populated backend is wired into CI.

AUTHORING NOTE: hand-authored from source (page.tsx + CommitLineage.tsx + CommitSessionChip.tsx), NOT from a live UI Bridge snapshot — the page is brand new and no instrumented snapshot was available in this session. Criteria use role+text (deterministic from the rendered tag + textContent per qontinui-runner/crates/spec-check/src/derive.rs) and the page container's data-ui-bridge-id. To promote this spec to canonical, run /update-spec commits against a live, signed-in qontinui-web with lineage data present and reconcile the auto-derived synthetic ids (heading-1-commit-lineage, the Refresh button id, the columnheader cell ids) against the snapshot, then push the populated-table state past the >=0.8 match bar.

states.name: Page header
states.description: Top-of-page banner — always rendered regardless of backend availability or how much lineage data exists. The page container carries data-ui-bridge-id='commits.page'; the h1 reads 'Commit Lineage' and the subtitle paragraph anchors the page's purpose. All three are present before any data resolves.

assertions.description: Page container (data-ui-bridge-id='commits.page') mounts — the most reliable anchor, present before data resolves.
h1 'Commit Lineage' heading anchors the page.
Subtitle paragraph describes the page (session-to-commit attribution); rendered with the header before data resolves.

criteria.text_contains: Which Claude Code session produced which commit

states.name: Toolbar
states.description: The lineage toolbar above the stat tiles: a last-updated text span and the 'Refresh' button (shadcn Button -> <button>). The Refresh button is always rendered (it only toggles disabled/spinner while a fetch is in flight), so it is a stable data-independent anchor.

assertions.description: 'Refresh' button triggers a manual re-fetch of stats + recent commits; always present.

states.name: Stat tiles
states.description: Four summary tiles (Commits / Sessions / Repos / By source) rendered in shadcn Cards. The tiles render their label text immediately (values fall back to an em dash before data resolves), so the four uppercase labels are data-independent. Labels are plain generic <div>s (no ARIA role), so they are matched by text content only — kept at warning severity because generic-div text matching is less selective than a role+text anchor.

states.name: Section cards (data-independent titles)
states.description: The 'Top sessions' and 'Recent commits' Card titles render regardless of data — the empty-state copy lives inside the same Cards. CardTitle renders a generic <div> (no ARIA role), so these are matched by text content; kept at warning severity for the same generic-div reason as the stat tiles.

states.name: Lineage tables (populated)
states.description: When the backend returns >=1 session and >=1 commit, the 'Top sessions' and 'Recent commits' Cards render real <table>s with column-header (<th> -> columnheader) cells. These column headers are structural (not data) but only exist at >=1 row, so they live in their own state. recommend_state selects this state only when the live page actually rendered the tables — at the hermetic empty DB used by Spec-CI, the component shows plain empty-state <div>s instead and this state simply does not match, so it never forces a CI failure. Assertions are on header text only — never on row content (commit shas, repo names, session ids all drift per run).

assertions.description: 'Session' column header in the Top sessions table.
'Last commit' column header in the Top sessions table.
'Commit' column header in the Recent commits table.
'Branch' column header in the Recent commits table.
'Source' column header in the Recent commits table.
'When' column header in the Recent commits table.

states.name: Session commits dialog (open)
states.description: Opened by clicking a CommitSessionChip pill. Renders a shadcn Dialog (<dialog>/role=dialog) whose title is the session label and whose body lists that session's commits in a table (Commit / Repo / Branch / PR / When). Interaction-gated — only reachable after a chip click against a populated backend, so no transition is authored here (a chip target has no stable role/text anchor, and the update-spec rule says transitions must be validated against a live backend before shipping). The dialog open-flow is covered by CommitSessionChip component tests rather than the transition executor. Asserts only the dialog container + its 'Repo' column header (structure, not commit rows).

assertions.description: The session-commits dialog container is present after a chip is clicked.
'Repo' column header in the dialog's commit table.
